entry.title: Re: Warm welcome from a UX strategist
entry.content:
Of course! For me, decision confidence is a key measure of success and ROI. I'm less interested in the AI capability itself, and more concerned in if a user a) feels confident in trusting the AIs content, and b) how they act in response. 

One of the common challenges i'm observing is around trust. There's a spectrum of users either trusting the AI answers too much and no longer applying any critical thinking, which becomes a business risk in certain contexts, to users at the other end who are approaching it with a mindset of distrust and sticking with manual or outdated processes. Then there's the goldilocks zone in the middle where I'm seeing users highly engaged with AI and are operating with enough domain knowledge to ask the right questions, they're treating AI as a decision-supporting tool rather than a blind decision-making one. 

There's a lot of opportunity in this space to add real value to both the business and our users, especially with reducing cognitive load, repetitive tasks, and the need to work across fragmented internal systems just to access information faster. The key is that the users remain accountable and have enough context to validate any AI recommendations before acting on them.

It might also be worth saying that i'm looking beyond quantitative metrics like adoption and utilisation to determine success, and primarily focusing on qualitative insights. Saying someone engaged with an AI agent 100 times doesn’t show if it delivered value, but saying they were able to analyse data faster or complete tasks in fewer steps is gold.

entry.title: Digital Payment Addon V2- Stripe V2 PSP- External Token & reauthorisation -Use Case
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation and need guidance on the recommended payment design for web portal sales orders using SAP Digital Payments add-on with Stripe as the payment service provider.

Main question: Can SAP Digital Payments with the Stripe V2 adapter support a flow where an externally created Stripe payment method token is registered through Payment Card Preparation, stored as an SAP Digital Payments card reference, and later used by SAP to create authorizations and captures for partial deliveries?

Scenario overview

Current process: MarketTime, the web portal, generates a file containing sales order details and the payment card token for each order. After the order is created in SAP Business One, the credit team uses the token from the MarketTime file to capture payment when the order is shipped. This can involve multiple deliveries against the same sales order. The current payment service provider is Authorize.net.

Proposed target process: Stripe will replace Authorize.net as the payment service provider, and SAP Digital Payments will be used for payment card registration, authorization, settlement, and reconciliation.

  1. SAP CPI will process the file to create Sales order in S4 Public cloud.
  2. SAP CPI will register the Stripe payment method token in SAP Digital Payments using the Payment Card Preparation process. The token will originate from Stripe and will be provided in the MarketTime file before the SAP sales order is created.
  3. The SAP Sales Order will store the resulting SAP Digital Payment card reference.
  4. Authorization will be performed later from SAP when the order is ready for fulfilment.
  5. Settlement and reconciliation will be performed using standard SAP Digital Payments processes.

We would appreciate confirmation on the following points:

  1. External Token Registration and Authorization

Can SAP Digital Payments (Stripe V2 adapter) create a payment authorization using a payment method token that originated outside SAP and was subsequently registered in SAP Digital Payments using the Payment Card Preparation process?

Proposed flow:

MarketTime → Stripe Token Creation → Payment Card Preparation → SAP DPA Card Reference → Authorization from SAP → Settlement

 

  1. Authorization Creation Using Stored Card Token

After a payment card has been registered in SAP Digital Payments via Payment Card Preparation, can SAP create a new payment authorization against that stored card token using the Stripe V2 adapter?

 

  1. Expired Authorization Handling

If an authorization created through SAP Digital Payments subsequently expires before settlement:

  • Does the Stripe V2 adapter support reauthorization of the existing authorization?
    • If reauthorization is not supported, can SAP Digital Payments create a completely new authorization using the stored payment card token?
  1. Partial Shipment / Backorder Scenarios

For orders that ship in multiple deliveries:

  • Can SAP perform multiple captures against a single authorization (multicapture)?
    • If the original authorization expires before all deliveries are shipped, can SAP create a replacement authorization using the stored payment card token?
    • Are there any Stripe V2 limitations for partial shipment or backorder scenarios?
  1. Strong Customer Authentication (SCA)

Are there any Stripe V2 limitations related to Strong Customer Authentication when:

  • Creating a new authorization from a previously registered card token?
    • Creating a replacement authorization after an authorization has expired?
  1. Recommended SAP Design

For the proposed MarketTime scenario, would SAP recommend:

  1. Payment Card Preparation → Authorization from SAP → Settlement from SAP

or

  1. MarketTime / Stripe authorization externally → Authorization Preparation → Settlement from SAP

We are trying to determine the most supportable approach for MarketTime orders while minimizing manual payment processing and avoiding issues caused by authorization expiry.

Thanks,

Sagar

SAC Excel Add-in Filter support is basic and mostly on member selection or exclude. entry.title: Re: Exploring Application Frontend Service: Deploying the UI of an MTA App
entry.content:
Hi Mio

Thanks for the blog. Some updates for reference 

  1. The workaround you described above to generate the deployment configuration for the HTML5 Repo and then change them to AFS is not needed. The CDS CLI now supports 'cds add app-front' which adds corresponding entry to the package.json file and later when you 'cds add mta' will add the needed setup for AFS in the mta.yaml file
  2.  The issue with redeploying application with afctl that overwrite the app configs is fixed. If no config.json is provided in the deployment, all configurations will be taken from the previous deployment

entry.title: Re: German B2B E-Invoicing Mandate - Current State for SA...
entry.content:
Hello Mike,

In general, for E-Invoicing - here are the approaches that we have considered
1) Via Peppol - using  SAP DRC ( Document and reporting compliance) -- this is a additional Licences and subscription that is required to be set up on SAP BTP. withing BTP, enable DRC. and Once DRC is set up, activate peppol exchange service.
This is supported as standard / suggested option. this will be automated approach meaning invoice created in ByDesign is then automatically created on DRC / peppol and sent to tax authorities.

Check : Integration with Peppol Exchange | SAP Help Portal and Integrating the Cloud Edition with SAP Business ByDesign | SAP Help Portal



2) XSLT -
Blogs you mentioned, Provides the set up steps and template that you can use.  This template XSLT files are good starting point to customize further for the specific needs.
We have few customers who are using XSLT transformations for the German E-invoicing mandate. however we have used the template and customized them for the customer needs and created a custom XSLT files that can be used for the German E-invoicing.

This approach is manual. meaning when invoice is created, system generates XML file that can be manually downloaded and imported to tax authority. ( and if needed, Along side the XML, you can also generate standard PDF as well).


looking at overall stretegy, being SAP Bydesign is in maintenance mode,  XSLT ( low volume and manual approach is ok with manual maintenance when rules or structure changes for e-invoicing), DRC/ Peppol ( high volume and automated approach) or third part add on ( Some external third party tool supports German and other countries too, and if there is need for e-invoicing for other countries - it could be viable option)

Let me know if you need further details

Thanks,
Harshal

entry.title: Automatic Packing Proposal in MIGO (Movement Type 311 to a WM-Managed Warehouse)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working in a scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where a stock transfer using movement type 311 is performed from a warehouse managed only in IM (Inventory Management) to a destination warehouse managed by WM.

The process is synchronous and the stock transfer is executed through MIGO.

My objective is to have the system automatically provide a packing proposal at the time of receipt in the destination warehouse, based on predefined packing specifications or packing rules.

However, I have not been able to configure a packing specification that is considered during this process.

My questions are:

  1. Is the use of automatic packing proposals supported in this specific scenario of a movement type 311 stock transfer between an IM-managed warehouse and a WM/EWM-managed warehouse in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  2. Is there any standard configuration that allows the system to automatically generate a packing proposal during the receipt process?
  3. If this is not supported in the standard solution, what would be the recommended alternative to achieve automatic packing suggestions in this type of process?

Any guidance, experience, or relevant documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Leave credit based on work attendance ratio in SAP Negative time management
entry.content:
Hi Team, Need help in configuring the below requirement in SAP HCM Negative Time Management.

1. Annual Leave Entitlement: 11–25 working days based on length of service.
2. Employees with Service Longer than 12 Months: Annual leave will continue to be provided on a yearly basis.
3.Employees with Service Shorter than 12 Months: Leave will be provided at the end of each month, provided the employee has full attendance for that month.
4. Attendance-Based Credit in Second Year: Employees will be credited annual leave during the second year based on their attendance in the first year.
5. Example:
Employee joins on 21 January 2023.
From 21 January 2023 to 21 January 2024, leave is provided at the end of each month, provided the employee has full attendance for that month.
At the start of the second year (21 January 2024):
If overall attendance in the first year is 80% or more, 15 days of paid annual leave will be credited.
If attendance is less than 80%, leave will be recalculated at 1 day per month of full attendance.
Any excess leave already used may be subject to wage deduction, but only with the employee’s written consent.

6. Attendance Calculation Exclusions: The following will not count as attendance when calculating annual leave:
Loss of Pay (LOP) days
Family Care Leave

SAP R/3 HCM Time Management SAP Payroll and Time Change Management

entry.title: Announcing iWorkshop - A SAP Community Meetup Focused on SAP BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
The SAP Integration landscape is evolving faster than ever. Modern enterprises are no longer integrating only SAP systems they're connecting SAP with CRM platforms, HR solutions, e-commerce applications, collaboration tools, AI services, cloud platforms, and countless third-party applications.

Choosing the right integration approach, selecting the appropriate adapter, and designing scalable connectivity patterns have become essential skills for every integration professional.

With that in mind, we're excited to announce iWorkshop – A Practitioner Session on SAP 3rd Party Connectivity, an interactive SAP Community event dedicated to SAP BTP Integration Suite.

Unlike traditional technical sessions, iWorkshop is designed around one simple idea:

The best way to learn integration is by experiencing it.

What makes iWorkshop different?

This isn't a conference where you spend hours watching presentations.

It's an interactive, community-driven experience where participants will learn from real-world scenarios, collaborate with fellow professionals, and put their integration knowledge to the test.

Practitioner Sessions

Hear directly from integration practitioners as they share real customer experiences, best practices, and practical guidance on building third-party integrations using SAP BTP Integration Suite.

Topics include:

  • Third-Party Connectivity with SAP Integration Suite

  • HTTP Adapter vs. Application Adapters – Choosing the right adapter for the right scenario

  • Practical architecture considerations and implementation tips

The Adapter Files – Scavenger Hunt

Learning doesn't have to stop at slides.

Our signature workshop activity, The Adapter Files, transforms learning into an interactive team challenge.

Participants will work together to solve clues, identify the right adapters, and tackle integration scenarios inspired by real-world connectivity challenges. Along the way, you'll sharpen your problem-solving skills, exchange ideas with teammates, and enjoy some friendly competition.

And yes... the best adapter architect in the room won't leave empty-handed!

Connect with the SAP Integration Community

One of the greatest strengths of the SAP Community is the people behind it.

Whether you're an experienced integration architect or just starting your journey with SAP BTP Integration Suite, iWorkshop offers a fantastic opportunity to connect with developers, architects, consultants, and integration enthusiasts who share the same passion for enterprise integration.

Every conversation is an opportunity to learn something new.

Event Details

Date: 8 August 2026

Time: 8:30 AM – 1:30 PM

Venue: Rojo Integrations, WeWork, Eleven West, Pancard Road, Baner, Pune

Registration: https://www.eventbrite.nl/e/iworkshop-by-rojo-integrations-tickets-1992624446359?aff=oddtdtcreator 

If you're passionate about SAP Integration Suite, curious about third-party connectivity, or simply looking to engage with the SAP Community in Pune, we'd love to have you join us.

Come for the technology.

Stay for the conversations.

Leave with new ideas, new connections, and a fresh perspective on SAP Integration.

We look forward to welcoming you to iWorkshop. See you in Pune!

 

Regards,

SAP Community Pune

entry.title: Requirement for Workflow Approval at Proposal Level in Automatic Payment Program
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

This is to inform you that our client has a specific requirement regarding the workflow in the Automatic Payment Program.

The client requires the workflow approval to be triggered immediately after the payment proposal is created and before the payment document is posted in the system.

At present, after activating Scope Item J78, the workflow is triggered at the Payment Batch level. However, this does not prevent the payment document from being posted before the required approval is completed.

Our client does not require workflow approval at the Payment Batch level. They have specifically requested that the workflow be initiated at the Payment Proposal level so that the proposal can be reviewed and approved before the payment posting takes place.

Kindly advise whether this requirement can be achieved through standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud functionality or if there is any suitable alternative solution or enhancement available.

We look forward to your guidance on this requirement.

Regards,

Shatabdi

entry.title: Re: S/4 Margin analysis: Posting Cash Discounts with profitability segment
entry.content:
Hello Max,

well it depends. Is document splitting for the G/L already active in the system in the first place? If not, then you would need to implement a separate implementation project on the subsequent introduction of document splitting, first. There is no shortcut to this.

If document splitting has already been enabled in Accounting, you should still be able to edit the controlling related additional split criteria, even if the system is already productive.

Of course this is subject to your organisation's testing and acceptance criteria. I would suggest to test it out in recent copy of the production system.

KR,

Steffen

entry.title: Re: Packing Instruction - Reference Material
entry.content:
Thanks, @Chris1973. I managed to get it working.

The solution was to adjust the "Define Procedure for Packing Instructions Determination" configuration and set "MIGO Vendor Receipt" to use the RMAT condition record.

After making this change, the packing instruction is determined correctly and the process works as expected.

Hopefully this helps anyone facing the same issue in the future. Thanks again for your guidance!

entry.title: Re: Filters not propagating to Data Analyzer across linke...
entry.content:
Hi,

I found a working workaround using SAC scripting.

Since the Data Analyzer currently drops filters that rely purely on Story/Page Model Linking, the trick is to force the direct filters explicitly on each chart's data source via script before the user navigates. Because they become "direct filters" and Data Analyzer picks them up.
 ...getDataSource().setDimensionFilter...

entry.title: Re: PO number should be available in the payment proposal...
entry.content:
Dear @S002476221520,

The PO number cannot be added at header level during payment proposal creation in the standard Manage Automatic Payments app. This is standard system design, not a missing authorization or personalization issue. For analysis or traceability the PO reference should be checked from the underlying supplier invoice / related documents instead of from the payment proposal header:

  • Use Manage Journal Entries and open Related Documents to see connected purchasing/invoice documents
  • Use Display Process Flow – Accounts Payable to view the document flow
  • For reporting/extraction use the CDS views I_SUPPLIERINVOICEAPI01 and I_SUPLRINVCITEMPURORDREFAPI01 to derive the supplier invoice and related PO reference. SAP lists these alternatives in the KBA

More details below:

SAP KBA 3772482 - Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item not displayed in Manage Supplier Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

IMPORTANT: Where the standard functionality does not meet the business requirement the correct approach is to submit an improvement request in SAP Customer Influence. This is the SAP-recommended channel for non-go-live-critical enhancement requests where customers and partners can submit and vote for product improvements

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@LauraHRC Hello -- I received this some time ago from an SAP Consultant in response the same question I had about Onboardees that were already in flight.  We have not made the switch yet because it scares me and I don't know what is going to break but will be forced to make the switch soon.

I asked your question about the inflight onboardees impact when enabling IAS and below what I found. Basically it says If they should be able to continue to use their original login method and it’s triggered based on needing access to PDC (Personal Data Collection). This sounds like if they have passed PDC step, then they will use their original login method. If they haven’t got PDC yet, then they will trigger the IAS creation.

Impact on Inflight Onboarding External Users When IAS is Enabled

When IAS is enabled for onboarding external users, the impact on inflight (already in-progress) onboarding processes depends on several factors:

Immediate Impact on Existing Onboardees

 For users already in the onboarding process:

  • Existing onboardees who haven't completed their process will continue using the original authentication method until their status changes
  • The extension status determines sync behavior: 0 (Active) syncs to IAS, 1 (Inactive) doesn't sync, and 2 (Pending) prevents sync even for active users
  • Users with "Pending" status won't be synced to IAS until their input is required (typically when PDC is needed)

Authentication Method Changes

 Before IAS Integration:

  • Onboardees received password reset links to set passwords in SuccessFactors HCM Suite
  • Login URLs included the pm_product_name=ONB parameter
  • Authentication was handled directly by SuccessFactors

After IAS Integration:

  • New onboardees are synced to IAS in real-time after completing the New Hire Data Review step
  • They receive IAS Account Activation emails to set passwords in IAS
  • Login URLs no longer include the pm_product_name=ONB parameter
  • Authentication is handled through IAS

Email Communication Impact

 Dual Email Risk:

  • If welcome email templates aren't disabled in HCM Suite, onboardees may receive two welcome emails - one from HCM Suite and one from IAS
  • It's recommended to disable the following templates in Email Services:
    • Template: (ONB) External User Welcome Message Template
    • Template: (ONB) Rehire User Welcome Message Template

Transition Recommendations

  1. Enable real-time sync to ensure immediate account provisioning and deprovisioning
  2. Update email templates to remove password reset links and include IAS activation instructions
  3. Configure proper home URL redirects for post-activation navigation
  4. Monitor the sync status through Admin Center execution dashboards

Sources:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3204536 - 3204536 - How to Setup up Identity Authentication Service (IAS) for Onboarding External Users – Onboarding

https://me.sap.com/notes/3281873 - 3281873 - [Onboarding] IAS Main KBA

Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Iden... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: How to add test actions about custom fields to a proc...
entry.content:
Hi Xuan,

This behavior is typically seen when the UI identification for custom fields is not unique.

A few things to check:

  • Verify that the custom fields are supported by TAT. Some custom controls or UI extensions are not exposed with stable automation identifiers.
  • If all three fields appear with the generic label "Text", TAT cannot distinguish between them and may interact only with the first matching field during playback.
  • When using Add Actions, ensure the label matches the one exposed by the UI automation framework. If the element cannot be found, it may indicate that the control does not have a unique automation identifier.

    Could you also share:

    • The business app where the custom fields are added.
    • Whether the fields were created using a Custom Control or another extensibility mechanism.
    • The UI technology used (SAP Fiori elements, freestyle UI5, etc.).

This information will help determine whether the issue is due to an unsupported control or a limitation in element identification.

Regards,
Lohitaksh

entry.title: Windows Upgrade - Connection/Driver Issues with OLAP/SSAS and Sybase ASE data base
entry.content:
Current App Infrastructure:

Windows Server 2016

SSAS/OLAP Server 2019 (Analysis Services only; no SQL Database Engine and used Multidimensional Model - Cubes, dimensions)

SAP Sybase ASE 16.x (16.1 SP00 PL02)

Existing cube connectivity uses Sybase OLE DB Driver 15.5 (Provider=ASEOLEDB.1)

Hygiene Initiative -

Planned migration from Windows Server 2016 to Windows Server 2019

During this upgrade, the current Sybase OLE DB 15.5 driver is not supported

Validation Performed:

Attempted OLE DB upgrade to Sybase 16 client :

Installed SAP Sybase ASE Open Client 16.0 SP04 PL06 in local Machine.

SAP KBA 2970913 confirms that the OLE DB provider is not included in SDK/Open Client for ASE 16.0 and above

Conclusion: no supported native Sybase OLE DB provider is available in ASE 16+ client stack

Reference:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2970913

Attempted ODBC DSN connectivity:

Created ODBC DSN successfully for Sybase ASE

In Visual Studio 2015 (SSAS multidimensional data source setup), ODBC DSN cannot be selected as a supported source

Microsoft documentation confirms ODBC data sources are not supported for SSAS Multidimensional models

Reference:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/analysis-services/multidimensional-models/supported-data-sources-ssas-multidimensional?view=sql-analysis-services-2025

Attempted Linked Server workaround:

Linked Server requires SQL Server Database Engine

Current OLAP server is Analysis Services only, so Server Objects/Linked Server option is not available

Therefore this workaround is not feasible in current topology

Reference:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/linked-servers/linked-servers-database-engine?view=sql-server-ver17

Can you please suggest on this or what is workaround for this.

 

@SAAdaptiveServerEnterprise

entry.title: Stop Tuning Your Database Manually - Let SAP HANA Cloud's Intelligent Advisors Do It
entry.content:
Managing a production SAP HANA Cloud database means constantly juggling performance, cost, and complexity. Tables grow beyond limits. Indexes drift out of sync with query patterns. Workload peaks spike memory and compute. Knowing what to change - and when - has traditionally required deep expertise and hours of manual analysis.

That's changing. SAP HANA Cloud delivers a suite of built-in functional advisors that analyze your instance and surface specific, actionable recommendations - directly from SAP HANA Cloud Central.

What Are the Functional Advisors?

The advisors are specialized analysis engines embedded in SAP HANA Cloud Central, accessible through the Recommendations card on any instance's Overview page. Each advisor monitors a specific domain - physical design, storage tiering, compute scaling, instance sizing - and produces recommendations with ready-to-apply actions.

Partition Advisor

The problem it solves

SAP HANA Cloud has a hard limit of 2 billion records per table. Large, unpartitioned tables also cause slow scans, data skew, and expensive delta merges. Knowing how to partition - which method, which key, how many partitions - requires deep knowledge of your data's shape and access patterns.

What the advisor does

It examines table specifications, row counts, query access patterns, and column cardinality, then applies a rule set that covers full-table limits, full-partition limits, frequent query predicates, temporal access patterns, and dynamic partition overflow. For each table it produces a single consolidated SQL recommendation with an importance level (High, Medium, or Low) so you know where to act first.

Key benefits

  • Proactively flags tables approaching the 2 billion record limit before they cause operational incidents
  • Recommends the right partitioning method (HASH, RANGE, Round-Robin, multi-level) based on actual data - not assumptions
  • Reduces the cost and risk of repartitioning large tables by getting the design right earlier
  • Works particularly well for very large tables, slow scan scenarios, data skew problems, and growing data volumes

Getting started

Enable the Partition Advisor from the Advisors tab. Provide credentials for a dedicated technical database user (not DBADMIN) with CATALOG READ and PARTITION ADMIN privileges. Default thresholds apply out of the box, but you can tune MIN_ROWS_FOR_PARTITIONING, REPARTITIONING_THRESHOLD, and INITIAL_PARTITIONS to match your operational standards.

Index Advisor

The problem it solves

Indexes that matched your query patterns six months ago may be dead weight today. Missing indexes leave performance on the table. Building the right index coverage manually means correlating workload statistics with table structures - a time-consuming process that rarely happens on any schedule.

What the advisor does

It analyzes all tables, existing index definitions, and workload statistics to identify two types of opportunities:

  • Create index — where adding an index on a specific column is expected to improve query performance based on observed access patterns
  • Drop index — where an index is rarely used or provides limited benefit, reducing storage and maintenance overhead

Each recommendation comes with a ready-to-execute SQL statement, so implementation is a copy-paste away.

Key benefits

  • Turns workload statistics into concrete index changes without requiring manual query analysis
  • Reduces both performance gaps (missing indexes) and unnecessary overhead (unused indexes)
  • Eliminates the guesswork of index selection by grounding recommendations in actual query behavior

Getting started

Enable from the Advisors tab. Requires CATALOG READ and the relevant INDEX privileges on the target schemas or tables.
Note: The current version supports filter queries on single tables. Join query support is on the roadmap.

NSE (Native Storage Extension) Advisor

The problem it solves

In-memory storage is SAP HANA Cloud's greatest performance asset - and its most expensive one. Not all data deserves to live in memory. Large, infrequently accessed tables or partitions silently inflate your memory footprint and cost, while small, hot objects may sit on disk unnecessarily.

What the advisor does

It collects access statistics over a configurable window (up to 90 days) and calculates scan density per object - tables, partitions, and columns - to determine data "temperature." Based on configurable thresholds, it generates two types of recommendations:

  • Cost recommendations: Move an object to page-loadable (disk) storage to reduce memory consumption
  • Performance recommendations: Move an object to column-loadable (memory) storage to improve response times

Each recommendation includes a confidence score reflecting how representative the observed workload window is.

Key benefits

  • Reduces in-memory footprint without degrading performance for hot data
  • Surfaces cost savings opportunities that are invisible without systematic access pattern analysis
  • Confidence scores let you prioritize high-certainty recommendations and defer uncertain ones
  • Supports bulk operations so you can apply changes incrementally and at scale

Getting started

Click NSE Advisor in Recommendation Card and enable the NSE Advisor in NSE Recommendations tab. Configure your collection window, hot/cold object percentage targets, and minimum object size thresholds. Requires TABLE ADMIN, PARTITION ADMIN, and CATALOG READ system privileges.

ECN (Elastic Compute Node) Advisor

The problem it solves

Some workloads are predictably periodic - end-of-month reporting, nightly batch jobs, seasonal peaks. Provisioning a coordinator large enough to handle the peak means over-paying for compute during the other 90% of the time. But manually identifying which queries to route, when to spin up an ECN, and when to tear it down is operationally complex.

What the advisor does

It analyzes past coordinator workload against configurable memory and compute thresholds to identify peak windows where query routing to a temporary elastic compute node would bring the coordinator back within threshold. The resulting recommendation specifies the ECN size (memory and compute), the workload classes to route, and a provisioning/de-provisioning schedule.

You can apply the recommendation in two ways:

  • ECN Policy (recommended): Creates an automated policy that provisions and de-provisions ECNs on your behalf - either on a fixed schedule (daily, weekly, monthly) or dynamically in response to live system load. The policy manages workload class routing automatically.
  • Manual: Add the ECN via Manage Configuration, update workload class routing locations, and remove the ECN when the time range ends.

Key benefits

  • Eliminates the need to maintain an oversized coordinator for occasional peaks
  • Right-sizing the coordinator for regular workload after implementing ECN routing can meaningfully reduce total cost of ownership
  • ECN Policies fully automate the provisioning lifecycle, removing operational overhead

Getting started

The ECN Advisor requires at least 5 vCPUs and at least one existing workload class. Enable from the Advisors tab, set memory and compute thresholds, define an analysis timeframe (30 minutes to 24 hours), and generate a recommendation. Enabling expensive statement tracing during the analysis window improves recommendation accuracy.

Performance Class Advisor

The problem it solves

Knowing when your instance needs to be resized - and to which performance class - is rarely obvious until performance has already degraded. Reactive resizing means business continuity risk. Proactive resizing based on gut feel means unnecessary cost.

What the advisor does

It runs automatically every Sunday, analyzing 2, 4, or 6 weeks of historical compute and memory utilization to determine whether your current performance class and instance size are still appropriate. When it identifies a mismatch, it produces a specific recommendation - which performance class and size to move to, and why.

Key benefits

  • Automatically generated - switch it on once, and it runs weekly without any manual trigger
  • Grounds sizing decisions in actual historical workload rather than estimates or guesswork
  • Proactively surfaces the need to upsize before performance degradation reaches users

Getting started

Enable from the Advisors tab and choose your analysis window (2, 4, or 6 weeks). No additional configuration required.
Note: The current version supports up-sizing scenarios only. Down-sizing recommendations are on the roadmap.

 

Getting Started with All Advisors

All advisors are accessible from the Recommendations card on any instance's Overview page in SAP HANA Cloud Central:

  1. Open your instance's Overview page
  2. Select the Recommendations card
  3. Go to the Advisors tab (*NSE Advisor is located in NSE App as of today. It will be fully integrated into Recommendation App soon)
  4. Select any advisor row to view details, enable it, and configure thresholds

You'll need the SAP HANA Cloud Administrator role collection to enable and configure advisors, and SAP HANA Cloud Viewer to read recommendations.

 

What's Coming — Analytics Advisor (Q3 2026)

The advisor portfolio is actively expanding. Analytics Advisor will analyze OLAP query performance across your instance, identify slow calculation views, and recommend creating MDS cubes - materialized aggregation structures that dramatically reduce response times for repeated queries.

Database sizing, physical design, storage tiering, and compute management are all moving from reactive, expert-driven tasks to automated, data-driven operations. Each new advisor is a step toward a database that tunes itself.

entry.title: Re: Material Group has not been defined error when using...
entry.content: The “Error in ITEM_IN_040000” is just pointing to the item line in your BAPI input. The real cause is material master data/config changes.  Either the material group was deleted or the material is not extended to the sales org/distribution channel. Fixing the material master should resolve the error.

entry.title: Supporting Engineer-to-Order Manufacturing with Variant Configuration in PODs
entry.content:
Introduction

Many industries, especially Mill Products, Industrial Manufacturing, High Tech, and Engineer-to-Order (ETO) environments, rely on Product Configuration (for example, Class Type 300) to manufacture products that are uniquely configured by customers during the sales process.

Each sales order may contain a different combination of characteristics, dimensions, colors, materials, or optional features. These configuration values become critical manufacturing instructions that operators must have available on the shop floor to build the correct product.

SAP Digital Manufacturing seamlessly supports the download and integration of these product configurations from SAP ERP, making them available throughout manufacturing execution. By displaying the configuration directly inside Production Operator Dashboards (PODs), operators gain immediate visibility into the exact customer-specific requirements for the order they are executing.

Instead of relying on printed documents, external systems, or manual communication, workers can access all relevant configuration characteristics directly within their execution workspace. This helps ensure every configured product is manufactured according to the customer's specifications.

Why this matters

  • Sales-order specific variability is the new normal. Customers demand individualized products; variant configuration ensures the order reflects exactly what must be built.
  • Execution needs clarity at the point of work. If operators cannot see the order’s configured characteristics and related work instructions at the station, quality and throughput suffer.
  • “Last-mile” integration is often the gap. Many manufacturers have VC configured in ERP but struggle to bring it into the operator’s workflow. PODs bridge this gap with real-time, contextual visibility.

How it Works

Create a Sales Order with Variant Configuration in ERP

Engineers and sales configure products using variant configuration and Class Type 300 to capture features, characteristics, and values tied to the sales order and its items. Sales Orders are then converted to Planned Orders, and afterwards to Production Orders, which are released for production and synchronized to SAP Digital Manufacturing to be produced.

Enrich Production Order Download Integration Workflow

To support the integration of the Variant Configuration characteristics, Class Type 300, the Production Engineer must customize the Production Order Download integration workflow, enabling <CustomFieldList> mapping to LOIPRO05 E1VCCHR IDOC segment, a sample is already provided.

With this customization in place, Variant Configuration characteristics will be saved to Production Orders Custom Data.

Display Variant Configuration characteristics in the Worker UI (Production Operator Dashboards, or PODs)

The Variant Configuration characteristics values stored in the Production Order Custom Data can be easily shown in PODs using different ways. Below you can find two different ways I implemented it myself.

Via Custom POD Plugin, check POD2.0-orderConfigurationData: A POD 2.0 widget that displays custom configuration data (customValues) from the selected order.

Features

 
  • Displays order customValues in a searchable, sortable table
  • Auto-loads when an order is selected
  • Export to CSV/Excel
  • Filtering and sorting by attribute/value

Design Time

Run Time

Via Action, check 20DisplayOrderConfigDataAction47: A POD 2.0 custom action that queries order custom data (customValues) for the currently selected order and displays it in a beautiful, searchable, sortable overlay popup modal. 

When assigned to a button in Manage PODs 2.0 app, this Action reads the selected order from the POD work list, calls the SAP DM Order API, and opens a polished dialog showing all custom key/value pairs configured on that order.

Design Time

Run Time

The action is triggered by the 'Start' button, which loads the 'Order Custom Data' popup after starting the SFC, exactly when the information is really needed.

Business Value

Displaying Product Configuration information inside PODs delivers significant business benefits:

  • Reduce manufacturing errors by providing operators with the exact customer configuration required for each production order.
  • Improve first-pass yield by minimizing misunderstandings and eliminating guesswork during production.
  • Increase operator productivity by removing the need to search across multiple systems or paper-based documentation.
  • Enhance quality and compliance by ensuring configuration-specific requirements are consistently followed.
  • Support highly configurable products without increasing shop floor complexity.
  • Improve traceability by associating customer configuration data with manufacturing execution records.
  • Reduce cost of quality by having less scrap and fewer line stoppages drive down cost of quality and increase on-time delivery performance.

Value Proposition

Bringing Product Configuration directly into SAP Digital Manufacturing transforms customer-specific engineering data into actionable shop floor information.

Instead of simply executing generic work instructions, operators execute the exact product requested by the customer, with all relevant configuration details available at the point of execution.

This enables manufacturers to confidently support high-mix, low-volume production, mass customization, and engineer-to-order business models while maintaining operational efficiency, quality, and delivery performance.

Conclusion

As manufacturers continue moving toward greater product personalization and mass customization, making customer-specific configuration data available on the shop floor becomes increasingly important.

By integrating Product Configuration into SAP Digital Manufacturing and surfacing it directly within PODs, manufacturers empower operators with the information they need exactly when they need it. The result is fewer errors, faster execution, improved quality, and greater confidence that every product leaving the factory matches the customer's unique requirements.

 

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Need to fetch value for custom field in Manage Custom...
entry.content:
Hi sappolar,

Short answer: Yes, it is possible — your approach (custom field in business context Accounting: Journal Entry Item + custom logic on FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION) is exactly the SAP-documented way to show the profit center in Manage Customer Line Items. The most common reason for empty values is that the logic only fills items posted or changed after the logic was published — it does not update existing open items retroactively.

Why the standard Profit Center field is empty

This is standard behavior, not an error. The profit center is written to the G/L account line items only, not to the customer/supplier line item, and one document can contain several G/L items with different profit centers — so no single value exists for the AR line item, and Manage Customer Line Items can only display fields with a one-to-one relation.
Reference: KBA 2901005

Is the custom field + FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION approach valid?

Yes. As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, you can create a custom field in the Accounting: Journal Entry Item business context and enable its usage in the Manage Customer Line Items app. SAP's own use-case blog shows exactly your scenario: an implementation of the Journal Entry Substitution (FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION) extension point that copies the profit center from the G/L line item into the custom field displayed in Manage Customer Line Items.
Reference: SAP blog – Custom fields for the Manage Customer Line Items app: use case and announcement blog (2402) and KBA 3405214

Why your values are not coming:

Most likely: you are looking at documents posted before the logic was published. The custom logic is applied to new and changed items only; to test it, select an existing line item, use the Edit Line Item function, and change the item — after saving, the field should be filled for that item.

Constraints to verify:

  • Field enablement: The field usage must be enabled (Enable Usage) for the Manage Customer Line Items app on the User Interface tab in the Custom Fields app, and the field must be published; only then is it available in the app's OData service and Settings dialog.
  • Business transaction coverage: FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION only works for direct accounting journal entries where BSEG is created; it is not called for CO-generated postings (e.g., settlement). Reference: KBA 3014271. It is also not supported in Manage Automatic Payments (KBA 2890482), and journal entry item substitution does not work for parked invoices — Reference: KBA 3328448.
  • Do not try to substitute the standard Profit Center field itself on the AR line: the profit center cannot be set manually (or via substitution) on customer/supplier line items, and substituting an account assignment that is a document splitting characteristic must be avoided (KBA 2810442). Copying the value into a custom field, as you did, is the supported pattern.

Alternative without extensibility

Use the Display Line Items in General Ledger app: select by Customer ID (optionally filtered by reconciliation account) to list AR line items including their profit center assignments.
Reference: SAP blog – How to Display Profit Center Assignments for AR/AP Line Items

Summary

Your design is correct and supported. Verify the field is enabled and published for the Manage Customer Line Items app, then test by posting a new customer invoice (direct FI posting) or editing an existing line item — historical items will remain empty because the substitution is not applied retroactively. For historical data, use Display Line Items in General Ledger instead.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Top Tips & Tricks for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Success with SAP Analytics Cloud depends on balancing performance, usability, and governance. By designing efficient data models, simplifying dashboards, leveraging AI-powered insights, securing data appropriately, and standardizing development practices, organizations can build scalable analytics solutions that deliver faster insights and a better user experience. Following these best practices helps reduce maintenance effort, improve report performance, and increase user adoption across the business:

Start with a Clear Data Model

A well-designed data model is the foundation of every successful SAC implementation.

Tips:

  • Remove unnecessary dimensions and measures.
  • Use meaningful names for dimensions and calculations.
  • Create hierarchies for easier navigation.
  • Minimize calculated measures when they can be created in the source system.

Benefit: Faster reports and easier maintenance.

Use Input Controls Effectively

Instead of creating multiple versions of the same story:

  • Add input controls for dimensions like Region, Product, or Year.
  • Allow users to filter dashboards themselves.
  • Link multiple charts to a single input control.

Benefit: Cleaner dashboards with better user experience.

Optimize Story Performance

Large stories can become slow.

Best practices include:

  • Limit the number of charts on a page.
  • Avoid displaying excessive data.
  • Use lazy loading where available.
  • Reduce the number of widgets that refresh simultaneously.

Benefit: Faster dashboard loading times.

Master Bookmarks

Bookmarks allow users to save personalized dashboard views.

Use bookmarks to:

  • Save favorite filters.
  • Preserve chart layouts.
  • Quickly switch between business scenarios.

Benefit: Improved productivity for recurring analysis.

Create Reusable Calculations

Instead of recreating formulas repeatedly:

  • Use calculated measures.
  • Create restricted measures.
  • Use reusable calculated dimensions.

Benefit: Consistent calculations across reports

Use Conditional Formatting

Highlight important information automatically, by using an accepted color schema:

Example:

  • Revenue below target → Red
  • Revenue on target → Yellow
  • Revenue above target → Green

Users immediately identify trends without reading every number.

 Keep Dashboards Simple

A common mistake is overcrowding dashboards.

Recommended guidelines:

  • Focus on one business question per page.
  • Use white space effectively.
  • Limit colors.
  • Avoid unnecessary visualizations.

Simple dashboards are easier to understand and maintain.

Use Templates

Instead of creating stories from scratch:

  • Build corporate templates.
  • Standardize colors.
  • Use consistent fonts.
  • Create reusable page layouts.

Benefit: Professional-looking reports with reduced development time.

Schedule Publications

Automate report distribution.

Examples:

  • Weekly executive dashboards
  • Monthly sales reports
  • Daily KPI summaries

Automatic scheduling saves manual effort and ensures stakeholders receive up-to-date information.

Secure Data with Roles

Always implement role-based security.

Examples:

  • Sales managers view only their region.
  • HR users access employee data.
  • Finance teams view financial models only.

Good security improves governance and compliance.

Use Data Actions for Planning

Planning users can automate repetitive tasks.

Examples:

  • Currency conversion
  • Data allocation
  • Copy versions
  • Forecast calculations

This reduces manual work and improves planning accuracy.

Monitor Performance Regularly

Regularly review:

  • Slow-running stories
  • Large models
  • Complex calculations
  • Frequently accessed dashboards

Continuous optimization prevents performance degradation as data grows.

Make Dashboards Mobile-Friendly

Many executives access reports on tablets or phones.

Design with:

  • Responsive layouts
  • Larger fonts
  • Touch-friendly filters
  • Simplified charts

This improves accessibility across devices.

Document Your Work

Maintain documentation for:

  • Models
  • Calculations
  • Data sources
  • Business rules
  • Naming conventions

Good documentation simplifies onboarding and future enhancements.

Productivity Tricks

  • Use keyboard shortcuts to speed up navigation.
  • Group related widgets for easier page management.
  • Hide technical dimensions that users don't need.
  • Use meaningful chart titles that answer business questions.
  • Apply consistent color coding across all dashboards.
  • Test reports with real business users before publishing.
  • Archive unused stories to keep the environment organized.
  • Validate calculations against source systems before release.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Creating overly complex dashboards.
  • Using too many calculated measures.
  • Ignoring data model optimization.
  • Displaying excessive detail on a single page.
  • Inconsistent naming conventions.
  • Skipping security configuration.
  • Not testing performance with large datasets.
  • Failing to document models and stories.

entry.title: Take Control of External Workforce Rates with SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Managing external workforce costs becomes challenging when rates vary across suppliers, locations, and roles. Without clear controls, organizations can face higher costs, inconsistent pricing, and limited visibility into spending.

A structured rate management approach in SAP Fieldglass helps bring consistency to workforce pricing through rate cards, approved supplier rates, and automated workflows. This reduces rate leakage, improves compliance, and makes savings easier to track over time.

Regular benchmarking, supplier comparisons, and spending analytics also help uncover pricing gaps and identify opportunities to improve cost efficiency.

To access Value Map Groups, you need to login to the SAP Community using an S-user ID or a Universal ID. Learn more and explore full document on the Spend Management Value Map, which highlights how sustainable savings come from embedding rate control into the hiring process, supported by governance, automation, and continuous monitoring. 

Also, discover the SAP Ariba & SAP Fieldglass Spend Management value packages which offer a structured and outcome‑driven approach that connects common business challenges to capabilities across SAP’s spend management portfolio.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps  

Beyond improving supplier compliance, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.   

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to our page - explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.   

About the author:  

Rachana is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over 15 years at SAP, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing roles. She enjoys working closely with customers to help them make the most of SAP Enterprise Support and drive real results.  

💡Have questions or feedback around this content? Don’t hesitate to comment down below or contact @Rachana_Rasania

entry.content:

Administration UI and Administration service:

Release

Test/Non-Productive & Productive Upgrades

2608

July 22, 2026

Variant Configuration service & Pricing service:

Release

Test/Non-productive Upgrade

Productive Upgrade

2608

July 22, 2026

August 12, 2026

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: Azure US West (Washington) (cf-us20)
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions
  • Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade
  • Pricing service – Extension Concept – Custom Formula Execution During Group Condition Processing
  • Pricing service – Expose Scale Base Information and Group Condition Indicator
  • Pricing service – ReadOnly Changed Document Items
  • Variant Configuration service – New AVC-Aligned Mode (Beta)
  • Variant Configuration service – Additional Filter Attribute for Field Knowledge-base Schema
  • Variant Configuration service – Class Node Specialization Improvement
  • Administration and Data Replication – Modern Administration User Interface

It also includes a call to act regarding the following changes:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions
  • Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade

Please check the updated Roadmap and Release Notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

Disclaimer

The information in this presentation is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP.
Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this presentation is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this presentation or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.
This presentation, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality. This presentation is provided without a warranty of any kind.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: Azure US West (Washington) (cf-us20)

SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing continues to expand its global availability with the introduction of a new Azure data center in US West (Washington) (cf-us20).

This new option allows customers with new contracts to deploy their tenants in an additional Microsoft Azure region, complementing the existing global footprint across AWS and Azure regions.

Key aspects to consider:

  • All tenants of a customer must run in the same data center.
  • Mixing infrastructure providers is not supported.
  • Existing tenants cannot be moved across regions or providers.

This ensures operational consistency while offering increased flexibility for new implementations.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions

Local extensions can read data from custom database tables via sap.db().execute(). In scenarios where the same extension is called repeatedly — for example across multiple pricing items in a single document — or where several different extensions query the same table, the same SQL statement used to be executed against the database each time, even when the underlying data had not changed. This caused unnecessary database load and added latency to pricing and configuration requests.

With this release, the results of sap.db().execute() calls are automatically cached at the SQL-statement level. The cache deduplicates identical queries within a single request as well as across requests, so the database is only queried once for a given SQL statement and parameter combination within the cache's eviction period of 60 minutes.

Note: With that, cached results may remain outdated for up to 60 minutes after changes occur in the underlying database table.

Action: Check your local extension implementations for database accesses and create a support ticket on component LOD-CPS if you require the caching to be disabled for your tenants.

Example use case:

Consider an extension that looks up a discount rate from a custom table ZDISCOUNT_RATES based on the customer's country. A sales order contains 20 items for different products — so each item carries a different product material number in its pricing attributes. Because the extension input differs per item, the requirement formula is executed for each item and not retrieved from the extension result cache. But as the SQL query which is executed by requirement formula 900 only depends on the country — which is the same for all items in the document — the SQL query is only executed for the first item and retrieved from the SQL query cache for all subsequent items.

function REQ_900(pricingInput) {
    var json = JSON.parse(pricingInput);
    var response;
    if (json.action === 'COLLECT_ATTRIBUTES') {
        response = req_900_collect_attributes();
    }
    if (json.action === 'PROCESS_FORMULA') {
        response = req_900_process_formula(json);
    }
    return JSON.stringify(response);
}

function req_900_process_formula(json) {
    var db = sap.db();
    var country = getAttributeValue(json.documentInput.itemInput.attributes, "KOMK-LAND1");
    // Query depends only on country — SQL query cache hit for all items after the first.
    var select = db.select()
        .columns("DISCOUNT_PCT")
        .from("ZDISCOUNT_RATES")
        .where(db.eq("COUNTRY", db.string(country)))
        .build();
    var dbResult = db.execute(select);
    var discountApplies = dbResult.getRowCount() > 0;
    return { result: discountApplies };
}

The SQL query cache is active by default and requires no changes to existing extension code. If real-time data accuracy is required — for example if an extension queries data that changes frequently — the cache deactivation can be requested from SAP via ticket on component LOD-CPS.

More information will be published with the July release in the Extension Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade

As part of our upcoming release, we will upgrade our OpenAPI specification from version 3.0.1 to 3.1.0.

For most customers, this change is expected to have minimal impact on existing integrations. Your current API calls and workflows will continue to function as they do today.

This update focuses on improving how our API is described, by aligning with the latest industry standard (OpenAPI 3.1.0, which is based on JSON Schema Draft 2020-12). It does not introduce changes to API endpoints or behavior.

However, if you use tools that automatically process our API specification, such as code generators, you may need to ensure they support OpenAPI 3.1.0.

Action: Check whether your code generators support OpenAPI 3.1.0 and upgrade if needed

New API specifications will be published with the August release on SAP Business Accelerator Hub for Variant Configuration service, Pricing service, and Administration service.

 

Pricing service – Extension Concept – Custom Formula Execution During Group Condition Processing

Custom pricing logic becomes significantly more powerful with support for formula execution during group condition processing.

Previously limited to item-level processing, value formulas (VAL) and scale base formulas (SCL) can now also be executed during group-level evaluation.

A new field groupProcessing is introduced:

  • true: formula is executed during group condition processing
  • omitted: formula is executed at item level

groupProcessing flag in extension payload

This enhancement allows:

  • More flexible pricing logic
  • Clear separation between item and group-level processing
  • Better alignment with complex pricing scenarios

Activation is controlled on a per-tenant basis via SAP support.

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition, Development Guide, and Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Expose Scale Base Information and Group Condition Indicator

To improve transparency of pricing results, two new response fields are introduced:

  • scaleBase: provides the calculated scale base value and unit
  • isGroupCondition: indicates whether a condition is a group condition

new fields in pricing response structure

These enhancements enable:

  • Improved UI visualization of pricing results
  • Better traceability and debugging
  • More accurate integration with downstream systems

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Pricing service – Read Only Changed Document Items

A new filtering capability enables retrieving only items affected by the most recent operation in a pricing document.

new items-changed filter

Example: GET /pricing/documents/{documentId}?$filter=items-changed eq true

The response returns:

  • Only items that were impacted directly or indirectly
  • A flat list independent of hierarchy

This provides:

  • Better performance through reduced payload
  • Simplified integration for change-based workflows
  • Improved efficiency for incremental updates

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – New AVC-Aligned Mode (Beta)

A new AVC‑aligned engine mode is introduced for beta testing in non‑productive environments. This innovation is a major step forward in enabling the native processing of Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) models directly within the Variant Configuration service.

What’s new?

With this release, customers can execute AVC-based product models without relying on AVC Forwarding, by leveraging:

  • A new knowledge-base schema
  • A dedicated AVC-aligned processing mode in the service

new schema 5 and new flag for AVC-Aligned Mode to use the new mode for this KB even with AVC Forwarding enabled

A key innovation is the introduction of service-side constraint compilation:

  • Constraints written with supported syntax are now parsed and compiled directly within the service using an integrated compiler
  • This enables AVC‑like constraint evaluation without dependency on backend execution
  • Classical dependencies such as procedures, preconditions, and selection conditions continue to be compiled in the back end and are delivered as part of the knowledge base

In addition, the following behavior is now aligned with SAP S/4HANA for advanced variant configuration (AVC):

  • All characteristics are treated as restrictable, independent of modeling flags
  • Full alignment with AVC inference logic and domain restriction behavior
  • Introduction of set-based semantics for multivalue characteristics, ensuring more consistent and predictable evaluation results
  • AVC syntax round() is supported in constraints

Business value

This innovation has significant benefits in both architecture and runtime behavior:

Reduced architectural complexity

  • Eliminates the need for AVC Forwarding and cross-engine communication
  • Enables direct execution within the Variant Configuration service

Improved performance and scalability

  • Faster configuration processing through service-side compilation
  • Optimized constraint evaluation and execution
  • Reduced dependency on back-end systems

Lower resource consumption

  • Reduced load on SAP S/4HANA by avoiding forwarding-related processing
  • More efficient use of system resources

Improved functional alignment

  • Consistent behavior across constraint evaluation, inference logic, and characteristic restriction
  • More predictable and transparent configuration results

Seamless adoption

  • No changes required to existing product models or user workflows
  • Unsupported syntax is handled gracefully with warnings instead of runtime errors

Availability note

This innovation is released with restricted availability as part of an initial beta program:

  • Available only for selected pilot customers that are using AVC in S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud, model in a VC-compliant way, and use the Variant Configuration service with such an AVC model without AVC Forwarding
  • Controlled enablement via tenant settings and knowledge-base configuration

More information will be published with the August release in a new document on the solution’s product page (Pre-Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Additional Filter Attribute for Field Knowledge-base Schema

The Knowledgebases API is extended with a new filter attribute kbSchema, allowing more precise filtering of runtime versions.

Additional improvements:

  • Correct handling of avcAlignedConfiguration
  • Consistent schema responses across knowledge-base generations

This enables better control and selection of knowledge bases in complex scenarios.

Example call:

new kbSchema filter

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Class Node Specialization Improvement

An important correction has been implemented for class node specialization in scenarios involving characteristics referencing MARA-MATNR.

The class node specialization depends on the assigned values at product level. For characteristics with reference to MARA-MATNR there often seemed to be no local value assignment. Then, the "default" value assignment used to be set, which is too soft for class node specialization. This has been corrected by:

  • Assigning values correctly with author “classification”
  • Ensuring proper specialization behavior

This results in:

  • More accurate configurations
  • Improved consistency with back-end systems

 

Administration – Modern Administration User Interface

The administration UI is modernized with a new technology stack using React and UI5 Web Components.

Home Screen

Knowledge-bases Screen
Benefits include:

  • Faster and smoother UI rendering
  • Improved responsiveness
  • Enhanced support for asynchronous communication

This update significantly improves usability and lays the groundwork for future enhancements.

More information will be published in the Administration Guide (Pre‑Production) with the August release.

 

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the 2608 release.

Please check the updated Roadmap and Release Notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

entry.title: Re: not able to activate connectivity service with connec...
entry.content:
Possible Causes

  • Wrong environment selected during service instance creation: The connectivity_proxy plan must be created at the subaccount level by selecting "Other" as the Runtime Environment and  not "Cloud Foundry". Creating it under Cloud Foundry will result in an error as mentioned in the Note 3289209 - Failed to create connectivity service instance with plan connectivity_proxy in BTP]
  • Multiple ServiceInstances or ServiceBindings created: In the Kyma environment, the Connectivity Proxy is provisioned only once into the kyma-system namespace. Creating more than one ServiceInstance or ServiceBinding causes inconsistencies and can prevent the proxy pod from activating correctly. [As mentioned in this blog]
  • ServiceInstance created via Kyma Dashboard instead of YAML: Creating the Connectivity Service via the Kyma Dashboard may not result in a connectivity proxy pod being created. Using the service YAML file is the recommended approach. [SAP Kyma using BTP Connectivity Service]
  • Cloud Connector Location ID mismatch: If the Cloud Connector has a Location ID configured but the application does not pass it correctly (via the SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID header or the CloudConnectorLocationId destination property), the connectivity proxy will fail to find the connector. [Connectivity-Proxy in KYMA not working]
  • Missing proxy configuration in the application: The application may not know the proxy address because the connectivity-proxy-info  ConfigMap and the XSUAA secret have not been mounted as volumes in the deployment.

Resolution Steps

  1. Create the service instance with the correct environment: In the SAP BTP cockpit, when creating a connectivity service instance with the connectivity_proxy plan, select "Other" (not "Cloud Foundry") as the Runtime Environment. For the service key, use "Create Service Binding" instead of the standard service key option.

  2. Ensure only one ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding exist: Verify that only one connectivity_proxy pod exists in the kyma-system namespace. Delete any duplicate ServiceInstances or ServiceBindings, as secret rotation caused by multiple bindings can cause proxy inconsistencies.

  3. Use the YAML-based approach for service creation: Instead of using the Kyma Dashboard, create the ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding using YAML files. Allow some time after creation for the Kyma reconciler (a scheduled job) to detect the resources and provision the proxy pod into kyma-system.

  4. Check and resolve Cloud Connector Location ID: If your Cloud Connector has a Location ID configured, ensure it is passed correctly via the HTTP header  SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID or the CloudConnectorLocationId destination property. If you only have one connector and no Location ID is needed, remove it from the Cloud Connector configuration.

  5. Mount the connectivity proxy ConfigMap and XSUAA secret into your deployment: Add the connectivity-proxy-info ConfigMap with the correct proxy host and ports, and mount it alongside the XSUAA secret as projected volumes in your application deployment. The proxy host should be set to connectivity-proxy.kyma-system.svc.cluster.local

    with port  20003 for HTTP.

  6. Check the Container Logs of the connectivity-proxy pod: If issues persist, inspect the logs of the connectivity-proxy-0 pod in the kyma-system namespace for detailed error information

  7. Ensure the ServiceInstance/ServiceBinding creation is not part of app deployment: This is a one-time setup and should be performed independently of any application deployment pipeline to avoid repeated secret rotation.

If it does not help please provide more details what are the exact steps which you performed and what is the error if you face any.

entry.title: SAP IDM: Read applications into IDM tables which have multi-value attribute(s)
entry.content:
In this blog-post, the example is of 'Reading Users from AD with the multi-value 'memberof' is considered. 

1. Pass type ' From LDAP  directory' 

2. Source tab: Ensure that memberOf is the attribute that is being read

3. Destination tab:  Lets say the main table is ZLDAP_READ.   In the target fields of this table, add a row such as Z_LDAP_MEMBEROF:GROUPS:NVARCHAR(500) with Source field as memberof. The datatype should be TABLE. Let's call this as child table.

Result: When the LDAP job/pass is executed, then the child table will contain all the group memberships for each user  while the main table will contain all other single-value attributes as configured in the Target/Source field.

If the Main and Child table is not existing then both get created during the 1st run  in the above set-up. If the Main table is already existing and the child table is added later , then the main table should be dropped . Re-run of the job will result in the creation/fill-up of both the tables

Note: Table Update to the Main table should be 'Add entries to table' , for users to be filled -in.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Overtimes to more time accounts
entry.content:
Hello experts,

@Frans_Smolders @Volker_Ruof 

I have a requirement from a client regarding the SuccessFactors Time Management setup. They need overtime hours exceeding 42 hours per week to be transferred to specific time accounts from which employees can later take time off (Time Off in Lieu).

The business logic for the weekly distribution is as follows:

  • Hours between 42h and 43h should be posted to the Flextime account.

  • Hours between 43h and 45h should be posted to the Overtime account.

  • Any hours above 45h should be posted to the Extrahours account.

Is there a standard solution or a best practice configuration to achieve this within Time Valuation?"

Thanks a lot

Jan

entry.title: Re: Material Group has not been defined error when using...
entry.content:
Hello @bigflatfoot 

Material group 01 has not been defined (V1 582) indicates that the material group assigned to the material is missing or no longer maintained in the sales area/configuration. Since the issue occurs in both BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE and VA42, it is likely a master data or configuration issue rather than a BAPI issue.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: [UTILITIES] New SAP Enable Now Simulations Added to IUT51 Explore SAP Market Communication for Util.
entry.content:
New SAP Enable Now Simulations Added to IUT51 Explore SAP Market Communication for Utilities in the German Energy Market 

We are pleased to announce that a comprehensive set of SAP Enable Now simulations has been added to the IUT51 learning portfolio, providing learners with hands-on guidance across key German utilities market communication and master data processes.

To access the simulations, please refer to the lesson https://learning.sap.com/courses/explore-sap-market-communication-for-utilities-in-the-german-energy-market/accessing-course-simulations-9.

Available Simulation Packages

IUT51 SAP Market Communication for Utilities – End-of-Supply

The End-of-Supply simulation package covers the complete process flow across supplier, distributor, and MaCo Cloud systems:

  • Business System Supplier – Searching for Point-of-Delivery in the Interaction Center
  • Business System Supplier – Starting Process End-of-Supply
  • MaCo Cloud Supplier – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Message Data
  • MaCo Cloud Distributor – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Message Data
  • Business System Distributor – Monitoring Process Document with Received Messages
  • Business System Supplier – Checking Point-of-Delivery and Supply Scenario

IUT51 SAP Market Communication for Utilities – Meter Reading Processing

Learners can follow the end-to-end meter reading process across market roles and system landscapes:

  • Business System Meter Operator (GUI) – Displaying Master Data and Entering Meter Reading Result and Sending Aggregated Message
  • Business System Meter Operator (Fiori) – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data
  • MaCo Cloud Meter Operator – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Aggregated Message
  • MaCo Cloud Meter Operator – Monitoring Mass Meter Value Processes and Navigating to Individual Transfer Document
  • Business System Distributor (Fiori) – Monitoring Process Document with Received Message
  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Checking Received Meter Reading Result

IUT51 SAP for Utilities – Master Data Germany

The Master Data Germany simulations provide detailed insights into the creation and display of master data for German market roles:

  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Creating Master Data for all Market Roles (Standard Consumption Scenario with MMD and SLP)
  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Displaying Master Data: Installation and MaLo with Supply Scenario
  • Business System Meter Operator (GUI) – Displaying Master Data: Installation and MeLo with Supply Scenario
  • Business System Meter Operator (Fiori) – Displaying MOS Billing Contract

These simulations complement the IUT51 learning content by allowing learners to explore realistic business scenarios and system interactions in a guided, self-paced environment. We encourage all learners, consultants, and partners working with SAP for Utilities in the German energy market to take advantage of these new hands-on learning resources.

entry.title: Re: Display Line Items in General Ledger App
entry.content:
Hello!

The balance app is designed for period totals by defined currency types (e.g., company code currency and parallel currencies). Document/transaction currency is inherently a line-item attribute and varies across postings for the same G/L account/period. Best practice alternatives for viewing document currency: Display Line Items in General Ledger: add the "Document Currency" and "Amount in Document Currency" columns, and filter/group as needed. Display Journal Entries:  search by G/L account, company code, period, etc., then show "Document Currency" and "Amount in DC." Manage Journal Entries: provides flexible filters and columns, including document currency at the line-item level.
